queue_item.action: Bind peer execution lifecycle events to target-authored, attested, trusted, signature-verified evidence packets.

progress.finding: An executed lifecycle event is necessary but not sufficient. Acceptance requires target-team authorship, one terminal occurrence, causal time consistency, execution-grade envelope evidence, exact attestation digest binding, deny-by-default builder trust, and positive signature verification bound to the builder identity.
progress.verification: 8 deterministic Node tests passed in an isolated runtime using byte-identical candidate code and repository dependency logic

queue_item.handoff: All teams should preserve scheduler acknowledgments as accepted-not-executed. A peer completion claim should be emitted by the target team and accepted only after assembling the exact evidence envelope text, provenance attestation, trusted-builder policy result, and independent cryptographic signature-verification result into this proof verifier.

reason: Shared queue state is updated through an append-only owned queue-update record to avoid overwriting other teams.
